# docuchat.app — full description for language models

## What it is

docuchat is a private, local-first AI for legal documents. An attorney (or any law office)
installs it on their own computer, uploads their own documents, and asks plain-English questions
across them. Every answer comes back grounded in and cited to the source: file, page, section, and
the exact verbatim span. The user verifies each answer against the cited source in one click.

It is NOT an AI lawyer. It gives no legal advice, draws no legal conclusions, predicts no outcomes,
and takes no actions. It locates and summarizes what the documents say, with verifiable citations.

## How it works

The pipeline is: ingest -> parse -> chunk + embed -> retrieve -> answer -> verify -> cite.

1. Ingest / parse: PyMuPDF for born-digital text (with page + character offsets), Docling
   TableFormer for tables and exhibits, Tesseract OCR for scanned image-only pages.
2. Chunk + embed: page- and section-aware chunks with source context, embedded by the local
   `bge-m3` model into an embedded LanceDB vector store (no server).
3. Retrieve: a hard matter pre-filter runs before similarity search, so retrieval is scoped to one
   legal matter and one client's documents can never leak into another's answer.
4. Answer: the local `qwen3:14b` model answers only from the retrieved context, under a strict
   grounded-and-cite-or-refuse prompt. If the documents do not support an answer, it says "I could
   not find this in the documents" rather than inventing one.
5. Verify: each cited quote is mechanically checked, character-by-character, to overlap a retrieved
   chunk's character range on its page. The displayed page and span are derived from the verified
   match, never asserted by the model. A fabricated or altered quote yields zero citations.
6. Cite: the UI shows the answer with source chips and opens the original page with the cited span
   highlighted.

## The facts that make it different

- 100% local: inference, embeddings, OCR, and storage all run on the user's machine. The service
  binds to loopback (`127.0.0.1`) only; it never binds a public interface.
- Private: the query path makes no cloud calls and needs no API keys. Documents are never uploaded
  to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or any third party, which preserves attorney-client privilege.
- Offline / air-gapped: after a one-time local model download via Ollama, it runs with no internet
  connection.
- Verifiable citations: mechanical, character-level span verification is the core differentiator.
  On our open evaluation, page+span citation accuracy was 98.4% (62 of 63) with 0 displayed
  fabrications.
- Free and open source: MIT-licensed, no license servers, no per-token billing, no subscription.

## How it compares to cloud AI chat (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini)

- Where documents go: docuchat keeps them on your machine (loopback only); cloud chatbots upload
  them to a third-party cloud.
- Models: docuchat uses local, open-source models via Ollama; cloud chat uses closed, vendor-hosted
  models.
- Citations: docuchat mechanically verifies every citation to a page and exact span; cloud chat
  citations are often unverified or hallucinated.
- Offline: docuchat works offline after the one-time model download; cloud chat does not.
- Cost: docuchat is free; cloud chat is a subscription or per-token.
- Privilege: docuchat preserves attorney-client privilege because nothing leaves the machine; cloud
  chat puts it at risk because data leaves your control.

## Tech stack

- Backend / API: Python and FastAPI, loopback-only.
- Local inference: Ollama, running `qwen3:14b` (chat) and `bge-m3` (embeddings), with an optional
  `bge-reranker-v2-m3`.
- Vector store: LanceDB, embedded, no server.
- Parsing and OCR: PyMuPDF, Docling (TableFormer), Tesseract, python-docx.
- Retrieval and answering: hand-rolled RAG (no LangChain, no LlamaIndex) for a transparent
  claim -> chunk -> character-offset citation path.
- UI: vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (no framework).
- Deploy: the desktop app wraps the local server; the container path uses Docker Compose and
  publishes `127.0.0.1:8000` only.

## Platforms and download

- Signed one-click apps for macOS and Windows are in progress; they will be linked at
  https://docuchat.app the moment they ship.
- Today: run it from source on macOS, Windows, or Linux (Python 3.12+, Ollama). See the README
  quickstart. The first-run wizard inside the app downloads the local models for you.

## Links

- Website: https://docuchat.app/
- Source (MIT): https://github.com/janderswag/docuchat.app

- Get it (run from source today; signed apps soon): https://github.com/janderswag/docuchat.app
